skill.name: DotNet Architect
skill.description: Expert .NET backend architect specializing in C#, ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework, and enterprise application patterns.
skill.summary: Senior .NET architect for building production-grade APIs, microservices, and enterprise applications with modern C# best practices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing architectural guidance for .NET development. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 security issues detected and a risk score of 0/100. No executable code, network access, or file system operations are present. The skill provides expert guidance on C#, ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework, and enterprise patterns without any security risks.

content.user_title: Build production-grade .NET APIs with expert architecture guidance
content.value_statement: Enterprise .NET projects often lack experienced architecture guidance, leading to technical debt and performance issues. This skill provides senior-level .NET expertise for building robust, scalable backend systems using modern C# and ASP.NET Core best practices.
actual_capabilities: Design and review .NET API architectures using Clean Architecture and DDD patterns
Implement modern C# features including async/await, records, and pattern matching
Optimize Entity Framework Core queries and implement Dapper for high-performance data access
Configure caching strategies with IMemoryCache and Redis distributed caching
Set up authentication and authorization with JWT, OAuth, and policy-based access control
Guide performance optimization using BenchmarkDotNet and profiling tools
limitations: Cannot execute or compile C# code - provides guidance and code examples only
Does not have access to your actual codebase unless you share it in the conversation
Cannot deploy applications or configure cloud infrastructure directly
Recommendations are based on best practices and may require adaptation to your specific context

use_cases.title: API Design Review
use_cases.description: Get expert review of your .NET API design including controller structure, DTOs, validation, and error handling patterns.

use_cases.title: Microservices Architecture Planning
use_cases.description: Design microservices boundaries, communication patterns, and infrastructure requirements for scalable distributed systems.
use_cases.target_user: Senior developers and tech leads planning system architecture

use_cases.title: Performance Optimization Guidance
use_cases.description: Identify and resolve performance bottlenecks in async code, database queries, and memory allocation patterns.
use_cases.target_user: Developers troubleshooting slow or resource-intensive applications

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Code Review Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this C# service class for best practices and potential improvements. Focus on async patterns, error handling, and dependency injection: [paste your code]
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want feedback on existing code quality

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Architecture Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a .NET API for [describe your domain, e.g., e-commerce order processing]. Include project structure, key interfaces, and data models. Consider scalability for [expected load].
prompt_templates.scenario: When starting a new project or major feature

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Performance Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this async method for potential deadlocks, performance issues, and memory leaks. Suggest specific optimizations with code examples: [paste your code]
prompt_templates.scenario: When troubleshooting performance problems in production code

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Migration Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration plan from [current state, e.g., monolithic EF6 app] to [target state, e.g., microservices with EF Core]. Include phases, risks, and rollback strategies.
prompt_templates.scenario: When planning major architectural changes or technology upgrades

output_examples.input: Design a caching strategy for a product catalog API with 100K items and high read traffic
output_examples.output: Recommended multi-level caching approach: L1 uses IMemoryCache for hot items (top 1K products) with 5-minute expiration. L2 uses Redis distributed cache for full catalog with 30-minute expiration and cache-aside pattern. Include cache key conventions, invalidation on write operations, and stale-while-revalidate for background refresh.

output_examples.input: Review this Entity Framework query causing N+1 issues
output_examples.output: The query loads Orders then accesses Order.Items in a loop, causing N+1 queries. Fix by adding .Include(o => o.Items) for eager loading, or use .AsSplitQuery() for separate optimized queries. Consider AsNoTracking() for read-only scenarios to reduce overhead.

best_practices: Use nullable reference types and required members to catch null reference errors at compile time
Apply dependency injection with appropriate service lifetimes (Singleton, Scoped, Transient) based on state and threading requirements
Implement structured logging with Serilog including request IDs and correlation contexts for distributed tracing
anti_patterns: Avoid async void methods except for event handlers - use async Task and await all async operations
Do not catch generic Exception without logging and rethrowing - handle specific exceptions and let unexpected errors propagate
Never store DbContext or scoped services in Singleton classes - this causes captured disposables and threading issues

faq.question: What .NET versions does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on .NET 6, 7, and 8 (LTS versions) with modern C# 10, 11, and 12 features. It can also provide guidance for .NET Core 3.1 and .NET 5 migration paths.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Blazor or frontend .NET development?
faq.answer: This skill specializes in backend .NET development including APIs, microservices, and data access. For Blazor-specific questions, it can provide backend API integration guidance but frontend-focused questions may need specialized frontend skills.

faq.question: Does this skill write complete working code or just provide guidance?
faq.answer: Both. The skill provides complete code examples for patterns, services, and configurations, along with explanations of why certain approaches are recommended. You can request anything from architecture diagrams to full implementation examples.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Azure or AWS cloud deployment?
faq.answer: Yes, for .NET-specific cloud concerns including Azure App Service, Azure Functions, AWS Lambda, containerization with Docker, and Kubernetes deployments. It covers cloud-native patterns but infrastructure-as-code may require additional specialized skills.

faq.question: What testing frameworks does this skill recommend?
faq.answer: The skill recommends xUnit as the primary test framework with Moq for mocking, FluentAssertions for readable assertions, and TestContainers for integration tests. It also covers code coverage with Coverlet and report generation.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for beginners learning C#?
faq.answer: The skill assumes intermediate C# knowledge and focuses on production-grade patterns. Beginners can use it but may need to ask for more fundamental explanations. It is ideal for developers with 1-2 years of C# experience looking to improve their architecture skills.
